description: AngularJS 1.x SPA — static files in src/main/resources/static/. Communicates with backend exclusively via HTTP.
human_reasoning: Pure static files. No server-side rendering. Communicates via HTTP only. Extraction is operational only: put files behind nginx, proxy API to monolith.

description: Album Catalog — AlbumController (REST CRUD), Album domain entity (JPA-annotated), JpaAlbumRepository
adr_reference: ADR-001 §Seam 2: Album Catalog Service — Risk: MEDIUM
human_reasoning: Core business domain with clear HTTP boundary. JPA annotations tangle persistence into domain model. albumId field has unclear semantics. Multi-DB profile switching complicates extraction. Business value justifies medium-risk extraction after characterization tests exist.

description: SpringApplicationContextInitializer — detects Cloud Foundry bound services at startup and activates appropriate Spring profile
adr_reference: ADR-001 §Seam 3: Database Profile Initializer — Risk: HIGH (do not extract)
human_reasoning: Not a seam to extract — a candidate for deletion. Exists solely to detect CF bound services. In containerized deployment, replaced by SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE env var. Touching it risks breaking the entire application at boot.

adr_reference: ADR-001 What We Chose NOT To Do §2: Not keeping ErrorController
human_reasoning: Chaos engineering test endpoints with zero business value. Security risk in production. Decision: delete from codebase, not extract as a service.

description: InfoController — /appinfo and /service endpoints serving Cloud Foundry environment metadata
adr_reference: ADR-001 What We Chose NOT To Do §1: Not extracting Platform Info
human_reasoning: ~30 lines of read-only CF metadata. Extracting creates a deployable unit with zero business value and adds operational overhead. Stays in monolith until decommission.
